Conversational English
with teacher Nanda
Questions
& Answers

🎯 Lead-in

Who is she?

Look at the photo and discuss the questions below.

1  Do you know who she is? What is she famous for?
2  What do you know about her life and career?

Read a little bit about her.

Simone Biles is a gymnast who was born in the state of Ohio in 1997. Biles has competed at the World Championships since 2013, and is now the most decorated gymnast in World Championship history. She has also won gold in the Olympic Games, and written an autobiography called Courage to Soar.
3  What questions would you like to ask her?
📖 Reading

Interview with Simone Biles

Complete each question with the correct question word or auxiliary.

Simone Biles
Q1's your most treasured possession?
AMy Olympic medals.
Q2What you want to be when you were growing up?
AA nurse.
Q3What you like about yourself?
AI like to have the last word.
Q4What your most embarrassing moment?
AI was on the podium at the 2014 World Championships in China when a bee appeared out of nowhere and took a liking to my flowers.
Q5What or is the greatest love of your life?
AFood.
 
Q6What your superpower be?
ATo be a witch. A good witch, of course. It would be cool to control things with my mind and do spells.
Q7 did you last cry, and why?
AJust a few days ago. I was overtired.
Q8 would you like to be remembered?
AAs a confident, inspirational, and very bubbly person.
Q9 word or phrase do you most overuse?
A"Oh my gosh."
📖 Reading

Reading Comprehension

Click each box to mark ✓ what is true for Simone based on the questions she answered.
She…

doesn't eat any animal products
tends to repeat certain expressions
is currently living in California
hopes to inspire people
is romantic
is very family-oriented
becomes emotional when she's tired
likes to make the last point in a discussion
📄 Interview – Simone Biles

1. What's your most treasured possession?
My Olympic medals.

2. What did you want to be when you were growing up?
A nurse.

3. What don't you like about yourself?
I like to have the last word.

4. What was your most embarrassing moment?
I was on the podium at the 2014 World Championships in China when a bee appeared out of nowhere and took a liking to my flowers.

5. What or who is the greatest love of your life?
Food.

6. What would your superpower be?
To be a witch. A good witch, of course. It would be cool to control things with my mind and do spells.

7. When did you last cry, and why?
Just a few days ago. I was overtired.

8. How would you like to be remembered?
As a confident, inspirational, and very bubbly person.

9. Which word or phrase do you most overuse?
"Oh my gosh."

🎧 Listening

Interviewing an Actress

Listen to some journalists interviewing a famous actress who has just arrived in Toronto. Write down the four questions they ask.

1
2
3
4
📐 Grammar

Analyzing

Click a question from the audio, then click its matching description.

Questions from the audio
Which question is an example of…?
1 What brings you to Toronto?
a a question that ends with a preposition
2 How long are you going to be in Toronto for?
b a subject question, where there is no auxiliary verb
3 Don't you like Toronto?
c a question that uses a negative auxiliary verb
4 Can you tell us if there's any truth in that?
d an indirect question
📐 Grammar

Question Types

Invert the subject and auxiliary/modal verb. With simple present & past, add do/does or did.
How long have you been waiting? Should we buy her a present?
Used to show surprise or check that something is true.
Why didn't you like the movie? Don't you have to be at school?
If a verb needs a preposition, it goes at the end of the question — not the beginning.
What are you thinking about? Who does this bag belong to?
About what are you talking? ✗
When who / what / which is the subject, do not use do/did — use normal word order.
Who lives in that house? ✓ Who did live…? ✗
Use indirect questions to sound more polite. Start with:
Can / Could you tell me…? Do you know…? Do you remember…? Would you mind telling me…?
⚠️ Key rules for indirect questions
✔ Use subject + verb order (not inverted):
Could you tell me where it is? …where is it? ✗
✔ No do/did in the second part:
Do you know where he lives? …where does he live? ✗
✔ Use if / whether with yes/no questions:
Do you know if Mark's coming? Do you know Mark is coming? ✗
💡 Also followed by the word order of indirect questions:
I wonder why they didn't come.
I'm not sure what time it starts.
I can't remember where I left my phone.
I'd like to know what time you're coming home.
📐 Grammar

Word Order in Questions

Click the words in the correct order to build the question. Click a word in the sentence to send it back.

Example:
tomorrow · can't · Why · come · you

click words to build the question ?

click words to build the question ?

click words to build the question ?
📐 Grammar

Question Word Order

Complete each question or sentence with the correct form of the words in parentheses.

Example:   Where on vacation last year? (you / go)
1
How often ? (you / usually exercise)
2
Who The Great Gatsby? (write)
3
Could you tell me how much ? (this book / cost)
4
I can't remember where my car this morning. (I / park)
5
your trip to Paris last weekend? (you / enjoy)
6
What kind of work ? (your sister / do)
7
Who the last cookie? (eat)
8
Do you know what time on Saturdays? (the swimming pool / open)
9
Why the present you gave her? (your sister / not like)
10
play your music so loud? (you / have to)
🗣 Speaking

Communication Practice

Read your card. Make indirect questions starting with the phrase in parentheses.
Then, take turns asking your questions and answering the teacher's.

🎓 Your Questions
1
What's the time? (Could you tell me…)
2
Where were the last Olympic Games held? (Can you remember…)
3
Is it possible to become fluent in a language just by watching TV? (Do you know…)
4
How many players are there on a baseball team? (Do you have any idea…)
5
How old are you? (Would you mind telling me…)
👩‍🏫 Teacher's Questions
1
Where did you grow up? (Could you tell me…)
➜ Could you tell me where you grew up?
2
What year were the Rio Olympics? (Can you remember…)
➜ Can you remember what year the Rio Olympics were?
3
How long does this class last? (Do you know…)
➜ Do you know how long this class lasts?
4
When did Germany last win the World Cup? (Do you have any idea…)
➜ Do you have any idea when Germany last won the World Cup?
5
Do you have any allergies? (Would you mind telling me…)
➜ Would you mind telling me if / whether you have any allergies?
⭐ Lesson Review

What have we learned?

📖 Reading

We read an interview with Simone Biles and practiced completing questions with the correct auxiliary verbs. We also checked comprehension by identifying what was true for her.

🎧 Listening

We listened to journalists interviewing an actress in Toronto and identified the questions asked — noticing different question types in real speech.

🗣 Speaking

We used the spinning wheel to answer personal questions spontaneously, and practiced indirect questions by transforming direct questions and asking each other.

📐 Grammar – Question Types

Auxiliary  Invert subject + auxiliary/modal verb
How long have you been waiting?
Negative  Show surprise or check info
Don't you like Toronto?
Preposition  Goes at the end
What are you thinking about?
Subject Q  No do/did — who/what is subject
What brings you to Toronto?
Indirect  Polite · subject + verb order · if/whether
Could you tell me where it is?
✏️
Homework
Questions & Answers
📝 Homework

HW1 – Question Formation

Right ✅ or Wrong ❌? Correct the mistakes in the highlighted phrases.

Example
A You have ever been to Thailand?
B Yes, a couple of times.
❌ Have you ever been to Thailand?
2
A Why didn't you tell me the truth?
B Because I thought you'd be angry.
3
A Where you usually go on vacation?
B We usually go to Mexico.
4
A Haven't you done the homework?
B No, I haven't. I'm sorry.
5
A What did happen at the meeting yesterday?
B We discussed the sales figures. It was kind of boring.
6
A Who's Jack going out with?
B He's going out with his best friend's sister.
7
A Who fixed your car for you?
B My brother.
8
A Whose jacket you did borrow for the wedding?
B My dad's. It was a little big for me.
9
A It's late. We should go now?
B Yes, we have to get up early tomorrow morning.
10
A For who are you waiting?
B I'm waiting for my brother.

Example:
“Would Michael like this wallet?”
Do you think ?
2 “Where is the elevator?”
Could you tell me ?
3 “Where did we park the car?”
I can't remember .
4 “Are there any tickets left for the concert tonight?”
Do you know
?
5 “What time does the game start?”
Can you tell me ?
6 “When's Anna's birthday?”
Do you remember ?
7 “What does Jamie do for a living?”
Do you have any idea ?
8 “Where does Natalie live?”
I wonder .
9 “What's Ava's boyfriend's name?”
I'm not sure .
10 “How much did you pay for your new car?”
Would you mind telling me
?
